title: Zelkova crenata
intro: Zelkova crenata, also known as Japanese Zelkova, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its unique, serrated leaves and stunning autumn foliage. The health benefits of Zelkova crenata include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Asian medicine, Zelkova crenata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions.
